location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in office; Wednesday optional WFH; Friday depends on role

secondary_archetype: Technical PM (mobile platform focus with people management)

level_signals: 10+ years of experience in Product Management
'Group Product Manager' title implies management of other PMs
Comp range 36,667–46,667 PLN/month gross, consistent with senior people-manager role in Warsaw

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 10+ years of experience in Product Management including 3+ years of people manager experience.
will bring over 7 years of product management experience, embracing the challenge of solving complex, ambiguous problems where strategies or solutions may not be clearly defined from the outset.
Systems thinker: You feel just as comfortable modeling data relationships in a technical diagram, just as well as you can partner with Design to walk stakeholders through a holistic UX flow.
Strategic: You inspire the company by creating bold, intuitive work connections within the platform and scoping them to solve the most critical customer problems.
Get Stuff Done: You create, flex, and evangelize a roadmap; thoughtfully break down projects to MVPs and iterative projects to ship with high velocity and business impact.
Customer-centric: You analyze our top customer personas; dig into the roots of customer needs; synthesize research to gain a deep understanding and narrow definition of a problem.

Communication and collaboration: You partner with cross-functional teammates to deliver high-quality results. You speak with clarity and write sharp product specs. You are highly comfortable getting into deep technical discussions with engineers about the pros and cons of different approaches, and you pair with design on complex user flows.
Grow Team Asana: You are a mentor for others; create great team morale among other functions and PMs; and share PM best practices you've cultivated to date.
Growth Mindset: You lead with curiosity and are open to feedback in order to learn.
Demonstrates curiosity about AI tools and emerging technologies, with a willingness to learn and leverage them to enhance productivity, collaboration, or decision-making.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in building in mobile for complex data models and visualizations, human-centric UX, and B2B platform or enterprise experience preferred.

scope.surface: Mobile product domain (mobile experiences and capabilities across Asana platform)
core_responsibilities: Create a product strategy to balance multiple priorities and use cases across customers, product teams, and enterprise-level compliance
Negotiate with partner teams, innovate in mobile and deliver a compelling roadmap that's focused on value for customers across our segments
Partner closely with Product Design to continuously improve the craft applied to our mobile products
Maximize clarity by bringing a data-driven mindset to setting concrete goals and milestones
Partner with engineering, design, and other functions to execute on roadmaps with high velocity

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as potential component of compensation package

green_flags: Explicit encouragement to apply even if not meeting every requirement
Clear in-office schedule expectations stated upfront (Mon/Tue/Thu)
Equity mentioned as part of compensation package
Rich benefits package including health insurance, mental health support, fertility care, and career growth budget
Asana is an established, well-recognized company (Fortune Best Workplaces 7+ years)
red_flags: Role requires Warsaw office presence — eliminates remote candidates outside Poland
JD mentions '7+ years' of PM experience in mobile description but 'About you' section states '10+ years' — internal inconsistency on years required
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not mentioned — unclear for non-Polish candidates
Compensation stated in PLN only; no USD equivalent or notes on sponsorship for international candidates

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Group PM', 10+ years, and people management requirement)
scope.business_model (inferred from 'B2B platform' language and Asana product context)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not explicitly in JD)
ambiguities: JD body states 'will bring over 7 years of product management experience' in the mobile domain description, but the 'About you' section states '10+ years of experience in Product Management'. Both have been captured; 10+ treated as the formal hard requirement since it appears in the qualifications section.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship eligibility are not mentioned anywhere in the JD; set to 'unclear'.
The 'Background in building in mobile for complex data models...' is labeled 'preferred' in the JD, so treated as a nice-to-have, though it is tightly related to the core role.
